# Mature Ideas

Mature ideas are close to becoming full studies, papers, or grant proposals. They should have a clear research question, plausible design, likely measures, and a next action.

## Popular psychology books and prosocial change

This idea asks whether reading popular psychology or self-development books can increase prosocial motivation, reduce polarization, or change how people relate to outgroups. It connects intervention science, narrative influence, identity, and public engagement with psychology.

## Identity-based approaches to polarization and public health

This idea asks how shared identity can reduce affective polarization and support cooperation around public problems. It connects social identity theory, public health communication, and intervention design.

## Reproducible workflows for social psychology

This idea asks how open science practices, R tooling, and transparent analysis workflows can make psychological research easier to evaluate, reproduce, and teach.
